query_normalized: quantifying hallucination in llm-generated api documentation computer science
rationale: The paper empirically measures hallucinated elements (e.g., nonexistent endpoints) in LLM‑generated API integration artifacts, providing a baseline quantification of hallucination in the API domain, which aligns with the same‑mechanism evidence (category a) and empirical baseline (category c) needed for a literature review on quantifying hallucination in LLM‑generated API documentation.
